questions.q: The Constituent Assembly was formed in which year?
Who led the Committee on Fundamental Rights?
What was the main reason for the delay in the framing of the Constitution?
questions.answer: The main reason for the delay in the framing of the Constitution was the differences of opinion between the members of the Constituent Assembly, particularly on the issue of the weightage to be given to the Muslim minority. The Muslim League had refused to participate in the Constituent Assembly, and the Hindus and Sikhs had different opinions on the issue of the number of seats to be allocated to the Muslim minority.

questions.q: How many members were there in the Drafting Committee?
What was the significance of the Objectives Resolution?
questions.answer: The Objectives Resolution, moved by Jawaharlal Nehru, laid down the basic objectives of the Constitution, including the establishment of a sovereign democratic republic, the promotion of social and economic justice, and the protection of individual rights and freedoms.

questions.q: Who was the Chairman of the Constituent Assembly?
What was the role of the Advisory Committee on Minorities?
questions.answer: The Advisory Committee on Minorities was set up to consider the safeguards to be provided for the protection of the rights and interests of the minorities, including the Muslim minority. The committee, headed by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, made several recommendations for the protection of minority rights.

questions.q: Who was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee?
What was the significance of the Constituent Assembly's decision to adopt the adult franchise?
questions.answer: The Constituent Assembly's decision to adopt the adult franchise gave the right to vote to all Indian citizens who were 21 years of age or above, regardless of their social or economic status. This decision was significant because it ensured that the Constitution was made by the people and for the people.

questions.q: How many Articles were there in the original Constitution?
